manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: swing, calf, horn, cell, stream, spine, bar, fair, straw.
Your opponent's team words are: cycle, poison, wave, hood, compound, alien, olive, missile.
Distractor words are: suit, bell, embassy, bear, point, state, shakespeare.
The kill words are: whip.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

TARGETS: <TARGETS>

If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

The user is asking me to provide a single word (only 1 word allowed now) that best relates to "stem" from the remaining list:
bear, alien, missile, suit, embassy, cell, whip, hood, poison, point, state, cycle, compound

Let me think about what "stem" could mean:
1. The stem of a plant - related to botany, biology
2. To stem something - to hold back, restrain
3. STEM as an acronym - Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics (but this is usually capitalized)

Looking at the remaining words:
- bear: could relate as in "bear fruit" but that's a stretch for stem
- alien: no direct connection
- missile: no
- suit: no
- embassy: no
- cell: In biology, a cell is related to plant structure, and stems have cells. This could work.
- whip: no
- hood: no
- poison: could relate to "stem the poison" (stop it), or some plants have poisonous stems
- point: could be "the stem points upward"? Not a strong connection
- state: "stem the state"? No
- cycle: life cycle of a plant involves stem
- compound: compound is related to chemistry, which is science, which relates to STEM fields OR a compound leaf has a stem

The most direct biological connection would be "cell" since stems are made of cells. Also, thinking about STEM education, "cell" is a science term.

Let me reconsider: "stem" could also mean the main part of something, like "stem of a word" (etymology), but I don't see words related to that.

Another angle: "stem" can mean to stop or restrain, as in "stem the flow" or "stem the bleeding". "Cell" as in "stem cell" is the most obvious connection! Yes, "stem cell" is a well-known biological term. That makes "cell" the strongest association.

So my answer should be: GUESS: cell
